---
title: "Getting Started with RDesk"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Getting Started with RDesk}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>"
)
```

RDesk is a framework for building **native Windows desktop applications** using R for the logic and HTML/JS/CSS for the user interface. Unlike Shiny, RDesk apps run as standalone executables with zero-port IPC, native menus, and system tray integration.

## 1. Installation

RDesk requires **Windows 10 or later**, **Rtools44** (or 45), and the **Microsoft WebView2 Runtime** (installed by default on most modern Windows systems).

```r
# Install from GitHub
# devtools::install_github("Janakiraman-311/RDesk")
library(RDesk)
```

## 2. Your First App

An RDesk app consists of two main parts:
1. An `app.R` file (R logic)
2. A `www/` directory (HTML/JS/CSS)

### Create the Structure

```r
dir.create("my_app/www", recursive = TRUE)
```

### The R Backend (`app.R`)

In `app.R`, you define how R responds to messages from the UI:

```r
library(RDesk)

# Create the app object
app <- App$new(
  title  = "My First RDesk App",
  width  = 800,
  height = 600
)

# Handle the 'ready' event from JS
app$on_message("ready", function(msg) {
  app$toast("Welcome to RDesk!", type = "success")
})

# Handle custom triggers
app$on_message("calculate", function(msg) {
  result <- msg$val * 2
  app$send("result_data", list(value = result))
})

# Run the app
app$run()
```

### The UI (`www/index.html`)

The UI communicates with R using the global `rdesk` object:

```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<body>
  <h1>RDesk Hello World</h1>
  <input type="number" id="val" value="10">
  <button onclick="calculate()">Double it!</button>
  <h2 id="result">---</h2>

  <script src="/rdesk.js"></script>
  <script>
    function calculate() {
      const v = document.getElementById('val').value;
      rdesk.send('calculate', { val: parseFloat(v) });
    }

    rdesk.on('result_data', function(payload) {
      document.getElementById('result').innerText = payload.value;
    });

    rdesk.ready(() => rdesk.send('ready', {}));
  </script>
</body>
</html>
```

## 3. Running and Building

To test your app during development:

```r
# Run directly from source
# Rscript app.R
```

To build a **standalone portable bundle** (a ZIP file containing R, your app, and a launcher):

```r
RDesk::build_app(
  app_dir  = "my_app",
  out_dir  = "dist",
  app_name = "MyCoolApp"
)
```

The resulting `dist/MyCoolApp.zip` can be unzipped and run on any Windows machine—no R installation required by the end-user!

## 4. Key Concepts

### Native IPC
Zero ports, zero firewall issues. Communication happens over standard input/output using a high-speed JSON protocol.

### Async Processing
Use the `async()` wrapper to run heavy R tasks without freezing the UI:

```r
app$on_message("heavy_task", async(function(msg) {
  Sys.sleep(2) # Simulate work
  list(status = "Done")
}, app = app))
```

### Native Menus & Trays
RDesk gives you full control over the Windows shell:

```r
app$on_ready(function() {
  app$set_menu(list(
    File = list(
      "Exit" = app$quit
    )
  ))
})
```
